confidence.label: Data Confidence
confidence.desc_high: High confidence in this MCA result. Based on robust, exact-match market baselines and significant live job volume.
confidence.desc_medium: Moderate confidence in this MCA result. Based on related market averages or a limited sample of live job listings.
confidence.desc_low: Low confidence in this MCA result. Relies heavily on broad economic baselines due to limited specific data.

micro.high: Your base pay sits in the {micro} percentile for {jobTitle} roles, placing you in the top tier of earners among your direct peers nationwide.
micro.mid: Your base pay sits in the {micro} percentile for {jobTitle} roles, aligning closely with the median market rate.
micro.low: Your base pay sits in the {micro} percentile for {jobTitle} roles, indicating you are currently compensated below the average market rate for your peers.

macro.high: Compared to the wider US job market across all industries, your salary is highly competitive, ranking in the {macro} percentile.
macro.mid: When mapped against all US occupations nationwide, your compensation sits comfortably in the {macro} percentile.
macro.low: Compared to the broader national average across all US roles, your salary ranks in the {macro} percentile.

regional.higher: Adjusted for {location}, local market baselines are roughly {diff}% higher than the national average, which is factored into your score.
regional.lower: Adjusted for {location}, local market baselines are roughly {diff}% lower than the national average, meaning your salary stretches further locally.

live.high: Live market data from Adzuna shows your compensation is strong, beating the {live} percentile of currently advertised US roles.
live.mid: Current job listings show active demand, and your pay aligns evenly with the {live} percentile of live offers.
live.low: Live market data indicates high demand for this role, with many current listings offering higher starting salaries. You map to the {live} percentile of live offers.
